message:
test(packs/containers/docker): per-pattern + per-severity coverage

Continues the kubernetes-pack coverage pass (acad49b helm, 0d2dec7
kustomize, 422a594 kubectl) into the docker pack. Same four-test
shape so future packs can be diffed against this one for parity.

What it covers:

- `docker_blocks_each_destructive_pattern` — exercises every
  destructive pattern individually so a regex regression names the
  pattern it broke instead of just "the pack matches `prune`":
    `docker system prune` / `--all`, `docker volume prune`,
    `docker network prune`, `docker image prune`,
    `docker container prune`, `docker volume rm <name>`,
    `docker stop $(docker ps -aq)` (the canonical "kill everything"
    one-liner).
- `docker_blocks_with_correct_severity` — pins Critical/High per
  pattern: `docker system prune -a` and `docker volume prune` as
  Critical (data loss is not opt-in for those forms);
  `docker volume rm`, `docker rm -f`, `docker rmi -f` as High
  (scoped to a named target).
- `docker_all_safe_patterns_match` — explicit positive coverage on
  thirteen read/observe/build/run-ephemeral commands so a future
  change accidentally promoting `ps`, `logs`, `images`, `inspect`,
  `build`, `pull`, `run --rm`, `exec`, `network ls`, `volume ls`,
  `info`, `version` to destructive is caught immediately.
- `docker_unrelated_commands_no_match` — negative space
  (`ls -la`, `git status`, `echo docker`) so the destructive regex
  doesn't accidentally extend its surface.

Pure test additions; no production patterns or pack registration
logic touched in this commit.
